name: Scala.js
description: Scala to JavaScript compiler enabling type-safe web development with full Scala ecosystem

description.value: Scala to JavaScript compiler enabling type-safe web development with full Scala ecosystem

active-maintenance.comment: Very active with 33 releases, 7,791 commits, regular updates through 2025

reactivity-model.comment: Depends on UI library: Laminar uses Airstream observables (fine-grained reactivity), Outwatch uses virtual DOM, scalajs-react wraps React

component-model.comment: Components are functions in Laminar and most Scala.js UI libraries; scalajs-react supports both class and function

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative DSLs for defining UI structure; Laminar uses fine-grained updates, Outwatch is fully functional

state-management-approach.comment: Laminar includes Airstream for reactive state; other libraries like Outwatch use functional streams

opinionatedness.comment: Scala.js is a compiler; choice of UI library (Laminar, scalajs-react, Outwatch) determines architecture

backward-compatibility.comment: Strong commitment to binary compatibility within major versions; careful deprecation cycles

primary-language.comment: Write full applications in Scala with access to Scala standard library

compiles-to.comment: Compiles Scala to JavaScript; can target ES modules, CommonJS

typescript-support.comment: Scala has its own stronger type system; TypeScript not applicable but JS interop is excellent

template-syntax.comment: Scala DSLs for templating: Laminar uses reactive streams, ScalaTags uses type-safe HTML builders

styling-approach.comment: External CSS or Scala CSS libraries like ScalaCSS; Laminar supports inline styles

build-required.comment: Requires sbt with Scala.js plugin to compile .scala files to JavaScript

reactive-primitives.comment: Laminar provides Var (read-write) and Signal (read-only) reactive primitives via Airstream

computed-values.comment: Derived signals in Laminar auto-update; map, flatMap, combineWith operators

two-way-binding.comment: Laminar supports controlled inputs with <-- and --> operators for bidirectional binding

event-handling.comment: Type-safe event handling with --> operator in Laminar; events mapped to observables

conditional-rendering.comment: child.maybe, display.none when, Scala if/else in Laminar DSL

list-rendering.comment: children <-- signal.split() with efficient element reuse in Laminar

component-slots.comment: Functions accepting child elements as parameters; Scala's flexible syntax enables clean slot patterns

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal; can be implemented but not a standard feature

suspense.comment: No built-in Suspense; async loading handled via streams and observables

error-boundaries.comment: Not built-in; Scala's type system catches many errors at compile time

context-provide-inject.comment: Scala's implicit parameters and context functions provide dependency injection

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: scala-js-dom provides typed DOM API; ref.now in Laminar for direct element access

official-router.comment: No official router; Waypoint is recommended for Laminar applications

router-name.comment: Waypoint by same author as Laminar; Frontroute is another popular option

nested-routes.comment: Waypoint and Frontroute support nested routing patterns

dynamic-routes.comment: Type-safe URL parsing with pattern matching in Scala

route-guards.comment: Can implement guards via Scala pattern matching and signal transformations

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Scala.js 1.16+ supports ES modules enabling code splitting

scroll-restoration.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented manually

built-in-state.comment: Laminar Airstream provides Var, Signal, EventBus for local and shared state

built-in-global-state.comment: Airstream observables can be shared globally; no separate state library needed

global-state-library.comment: Airstream is Laminar's FRP library for state management

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No dedicated browser devtools; standard browser DevTools work for debugging

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; implement via localStorage interop

official-cli.comment: Uses sbt (Scala Build Tool) with scala-js plugin; no dedicated CLI

hot-module-replacement.comment: Via Vite integration with vite-plugin-scalajs; sbt ~fastLinkJS provides incremental compilation

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated browser extension; source maps enable debugging in standard DevTools

vs-code-extension.comment: Metals extension provides full Scala.js support including LSP, completion, and navigation

error-messages.comment: Scala compiler provides detailed type errors; Metals shows inline diagnostics

documentation-quality.comment: Good official docs, tutorials, and Li Haoyi's Hands-on Scala.js book

playground.comment: Scastie is the official Scala playground, supports Scala.js

bundle-size-core.comment: ~45 kB gzipped minimum for optimized Scala.js output per official docs

bundle-size-full.comment: Varies significantly by application; unoptimized can be 20MB+, optimized with Closure ~100-200 kB typical

virtual-dom.comment: Laminar uses fine-grained reactivity with direct DOM updates; Outwatch uses snabbdom virtual DOM

compile-time-optimization.comment: Scala.js optimizer performs dead code elimination, property compression, and inlining

tree-shaking.comment: Fast optimizer removes unreachable classes and methods

code-splitting.comment: ES module output enables code splitting with bundlers; improved in 1.16+

lazy-components.comment: ES module support enables dynamic imports for lazy loading

ssr-capable.comment: scalajs-react supports SSR on JVM; can also run Scala.js output in Node.js

streaming-ssr.comment: Not commonly used; possible with custom implementation

partial-hydration.comment: Not built-in; would require custom implementation

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in standard js-framework-benchmark; Laminar performs well in manual benchmarks

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Distributed via Maven/sbt, not npm; scalajs-bundler provides npm integration

component-libraries.comment: Laminar, scalajs-react, Outwatch are main UI libraries; can use Material UI via JS interop

meta-framework.comment: No dedicated meta-framework; Udash provides full-stack Scala web development

major-companies-using.comment: Companies using Scala (Twitter, LinkedIn, Databricks) may use Scala.js; specific adoption data limited

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Originated at EPFL (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology); Scala Center provides support

discord-community-size.comment: Scala Discord (~9,164 members) has #scala-js channel; Laminar has its own Discord

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; Scala backend jobs are common but Scala.js frontend is rare

official-testing-library.comment: Uses Scala testing libraries: ScalaTest, MUnit, uTest all support Scala.js

testing-library-name.comment: Multiple Scala testing frameworks work with Scala.js

component-testing.comment: Full unit testing support; scalajs-react has dedicated test module

e2e-testing-integration.comment: scala-js-env-playwright provides Playwright integration for running tests

snapshot-testing.comment: Available via testing libraries; scalajs-react supports snapshot testing

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile solution; can use Capacitor or Cordova with web output

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; can be added manually with service workers

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop solution; Electron or Tauri work with Scala.js output

learning-curve.comment: Requires learning Scala, functional programming concepts, sbt build tool, and chosen UI library

concepts-to-learn.comment: Scala syntax, implicits, sbt, Scala.js specifics, DOM interop, chosen UI library (Laminar: Vars, Signals, Observers), routing

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~15 minutes with sbt setup; Vite template available for faster start

migration-path.comment: Rewrite required; facade types allow gradual migration of JS libraries

incremental-adoption.comment: Scala.js can target a single DOM element; embed in existing JS apps
